categories.label: Identity & advertising
categories.description: Prevent advertising tracking and profiling by Google, OPPO, and third-party ad networks.

checks.title: Delete the advertising ID
checks.risk: A unique identifier used by ad networks to build your profile across all apps.
checks.whyItMatters: Since Android 12, you can fully delete this ID instead of just resetting it.

checks.title: Turn off Google personalized ads
checks.risk: Without this step, Google uses your history to target ads across its services.

categories.label: HeyTap / OPPO / OnePlus account
categories.description: Limit data sharing with OPPO, OnePlus, or Realme through your account.

checks.title: Turn off HeyTap / OPPO account personalization
checks.risk: OPPO, OnePlus, and Realme share a unified account (HeyTap / OPPO Account) that collects your usage history to "personalize" services and target ads.
checks.whyItMatters: Enabled by default at account creation. On OnePlus, the OPPO merger means the same settings apply from OxygenOS 13 onwards.

steps.label: Open account settings
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > HeyTap (or OPPO / OnePlus) Account > Privacy

categories.label: Network & sovereign DNS
categories.description: Encrypt your DNS queries and choose an operator that does not sell your data.

checks.title: Enable a sovereign private DNS
checks.risk: Without private DNS, your ISP sees every site you visit, even over HTTPS.
checks.whyItMatters: Private DNS (DoT) encrypts these queries and routes them to an operator you choose.

steps.label: Enter the recommended hostname for your country

checks.title: Disable always-on Wi-Fi/Bluetooth scanning
checks.risk: Android continuously scans Wi-Fi and Bluetooth networks to improve location, even when you don't use them.

steps.label: Open location settings
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > Location > Location services > Wi-Fi/Bluetooth scanning

categories.label: Voice assistants
categories.description: Prevent permanent microphone listening by voice assistants.

checks.title: Turn off "Hey Google"
checks.risk: With the hotword enabled, the microphone is always listening.

steps.label: Open the Google app, then Settings > Google Assistant
Go to "Hey Google & Voice Match" and turn it off

categories.label: ColorOS / OxygenOS services
categories.description: Disable services that collect or transmit data in the background.

checks.title: Turn off system content recommendations
checks.risk: ColorOS, OxygenOS, and Realme UI surface app and content recommendations driven by your usage across several system apps.
checks.whyItMatters: Enabled by default. Turning this off removes most contextual ads in the UI.

steps.label: Turn off "Receive app and content recommendations"

checks.title: Turn off app store personalized ads
checks.risk: The OPPO App Market (or Play Store on recent OnePlus devices) shows targeted ads based on your install and usage history.

steps.label: Open ad services
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > Additional settings > Privacy > Ad services

checks.title: Turn off Smart Services
checks.risk: Smart Services ("Personalised services") collect your usage history to drive home screen suggestions and widget recommendations.
checks.whyItMatters: No functional impact when disabled: apps keep working normally.

steps.label: Open privacy settings
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > Privacy > Permissions and services > Smart Services

categories.label: Diagnostics & reports
categories.description: Stop automatic usage and diagnostic reports from being sent to OPPO, OnePlus, or Realme.

checks.title: Turn off the User Experience Program
checks.risk: The User Experience Program (ColorOS) and "Join user experience program" (OxygenOS) continuously send usage and error reports containing information about your device and habits.
checks.whyItMatters: OnePlus was criticized in 2017 for linking these reports to device identifiers. The program remains enabled by default.

steps.label: Open privacy settings
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > Privacy > Advanced > Usage and diagnostics

steps.label: Turn off "Usage and diagnostics" (or "User Experience Program")
Turn off "Send usage data" if present

categories.label: App permissions
categories.description: Check that no app has unjustified access to your microphone, camera, or location.

checks.title: Audit sensitive permissions
checks.risk: Some apps keep microphone, camera, or background location access far beyond what they need.

steps.label: Open the permission manager
steps.fallbackPath: Settings > Privacy > Permissions and services > Permission manager

steps.label: Go through each category (Microphone, Camera, Location) and restrict suspicious apps
